stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Distant strike on Russian missile ship in Caspian Sea
stories.summary: Defence ministers of Ukraine and Sweden talk Gripen fighter jet deal; verbal exchanges escalate over Victory Day parade. What we know on day 1,535 Ukraine’s military has struck a Russian Karakurt-class small missile carrier on the Caspian Sea off Russia’s distant Dagestan region , the Ukrainian general staff said on Thursday. The port of Kaspiysk where the ship was reportedly located is more than 1,500km from Kyiv – and closer to Tehran , the Iranian capital. The extent of the damage was being a

stories.title: EU meets to game-plan hantavirus response as WHO calms outbreak fears
stories.summary: EU countries met Thursday afternoon to coordinate their response to the hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship bound for Spain, which has so far killed three people. The World Health Organization, meanwhile, moved to calm fears that the outbreak could trigger a global health emergency on the scale of Covid-19. “This is not the start of an epidemic, this is not the start of a pandemic,” Maria Van Kerkhove from the World Health Organization told reporters Thursday. Hantavirus is a disease typically

stories.title: Royal Navy tracks Russian frigate for one month off UK coast
stories.summary: Moscow steps up maritime presence in North Sea after UK threats to seize shadow fleet oil tankers Britain’s Royal Navy tracked and followed a Russian frigate every day last month as it sailed from the Atlantic to the North Sea, as Moscow steps up its maritime presence after UK threats to seize shadow fleet oil tankers . The Russian navy’s Admiral Grigorovich escorted six Russia-linked vessels during April, including at least three under economic sanction passing east through the Dover strait, wh

stories.title: Trump’s tantrums over Nato are prompting European leaders to think the unthinkable | Paul Taylor
stories.summary: Europeans must urgently seek to close their security gaps in case Russia attacks – and the US refuses to defend its allies Donald Trump’s war in Iran and tirades against Nato allies are accelerating moves to develop a plan B for European security in case the US is no longer willing to help defend allies against a Russian attack. Europe must prepare for sudden vulnerability gaps if the fickle US president decides to pull out key military enablers before Europeans can develop their own alternative

stories.title: Hantavirus outbreak ‘not start of pandemic’ says WHO, as Canaries secure concessions over cruise docking plans - as it happened
stories.summary: Health body gives briefing as Spain says ship will not dock in Tenerife but be anchored offshore and passengers will have no contact with the public Meanwhile, Hungary’s incoming prime minister Péter Magyar has arrived for talks at Palazzo Chigi in Rome, where he is due to meet with Italy’s prime minister Giorgia Meloni. Since he is not the Hungarian PM yet – he will take his role this weekend – he was officially welcomed outside the Palazzo by a senior adviser to the Italian PM instead. Continu

stories.title: Southern Poverty Law Center pleads not guilty in federal fraud case
stories.summary: Prosecutors allege SPLC funneled over $3m to sources in extremist groups but legal experts say case is weak Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Southern Poverty Law Center (SPLC) pleaded not guilty on Thursday to charges related to allegations the organization committed fraud and conspired to money launder. The 11-count indictment filed last month accuses the civil rights organization of committing fraud in connection to a program in which it paid informants to monitor rightwin

stories.title: Trump may be toxic and Orbán is gone, but Europe’s far right is not in decline | Cas Mudde
stories.summary: Let’s not draw the wrong conclusions from Hungary’s election or the US president’s troubles Viktor Orbán’s crushing defeat in last month’s Hungarian election has led to an outbreak of democratic optimism. Across the globe, democrats are drawing lessons from the results and speculating about the decline of the far right . There is simultaneously a consensus that Donald Trump has gone from inspiration to “ liability ” for the global far right. While the fall of Orbán has great symbolic significanc

stories.title: Vilseck, Germany: A town on edge over US troop withdrawal
stories.summary: If US President Donald Trump is serious, 5,000 US soldiers could be withdrawn from Germany. This would include the Stryker Brigade in Vilseck. The town is in shock.

stories.title: Leszavazta a bizottság a szegedi plakátrendeletet, megvárnák a leendő miniszter, Vitézy Dávid javaslatát
stories.summary: A javaslatot a kereszténydemokrata Ruzsa Roland viszont megszavazza majd, miközben az elsősorban a Fidesz–KDNP plakátkampányai miatt született.

stories.title: Összeverekedtek a Real Madrid sztárjai, Valverdét kórházba kellett vinni
stories.summary: Tchouaméni és Valverde előbb a pályán, majd az öltözőben is egymásnak ugrott, példátlan válsághelyzet alakult ki a Clásico előtt.

stories.title: Kiadó a Nanushka budapesti üzlete, megkérdeztük a vezérigazgatót, miért
stories.summary: Havi 5,2 millió forintot kell kifizetnie érte annak, aki kiveszi a Bécsi utcai helyiséget.

stories.title: Leszedik Wáberer György nevét a róla elnevezett sátoraljaújhelyi uszodáról
stories.summary: A döntés mögött az állhat, hogy a milliárdos üzletember nyíltan beállt Magyar Péter és a Tisza Párt mögé.

stories.title: Távozik a Budapesti Metropolitan Egyetem elnök-vezérigazgatója, Matolcsy György korábbi kabinetfőnöke
stories.summary: Mai hír, hogy feljelentést tett az ÁSZ, miután kiderítette, hogy a METU-ból is a Matolcsy-kör lapátolta ki a pénzt. Dr. Czene Gréta személyes okokkal indokolta döntését.

stories.title: Rengeteg buktató vár Magyar Péterékre az EU-s pénzekért zajló versenyfutásban
stories.summary: A több ezer milliárd forint hazahozatala egy sokkal nehezebb menet lesz, mint első ránézésre tűnhet. A Téma legújabb adásában Pardavi Mártával, a Magyar Helsinki Bizottság társelnökével és Márton Balázzsal, a Telex szakújságírójával arról beszéltünk, sikerülhet-e minden EU-s elvárást időben teljesítenie a Tisza-kormánynak.

stories.title: Magyar Péter pénteken jelenti be, hogy kit jelöl Melléthei-Barna Márton helyett igazságügyi miniszternek
stories.summary: Melléthei-Barna Márton azzal indokolta lemondását, hogy a leendő miniszterelnökkel fennálló rokoni kapcsolat nem segíti a rendszerváltást.

stories.title: Klimatikus vészhelyzet lesz a nyáron Budapest főtájépítésze szerint
stories.summary: Lehet, hogy minden idők legpusztítóbb nyara jön a fővárosban – közölte Bardóczi Sándor.

stories.title: Vezető tiszás politikusok méltatták Melléthei-Barna Márton döntését
stories.summary: Gajdos László szerint Melléthei-Barna Márton példát mutatott demokráciából, Forsthoffer Ágnes pedig valódi rendszerváltónak nevezte Magyar Péter sógorát.

stories.title: Elővettük Orbán Viktor tizenhat évvel ezelőtti beszédét, ami életre hívta a NER-t, és amiben harcot hirdetett a korrupció ellen
stories.summary: A hatalomba 2002 után visszatérő miniszterelnök tizenhat éve, 2010. május 14-én mondta el az első beszédét a frissen megalakult Országgyűlésben. A korrupció, az érdemtelen állami kifizetések, az „oszd meg és uralkodj” legyőzését ígérte, miközben már ekkor az ellenük fenekedő „nagy erők” elleni harcra készült. A NER-t a régen várt nemzeti egység kifejezésének szánta.

stories.title: Már menthetetlen lehet az EU helyreállítási alapjának egy része
stories.summary: A Politicónak és a Népszavának is arra utaltak uniós források, hogy túl kevésnek tűnik az idő az Orbán-kormány lemaradásának behozására.

stories.title: Magyar Péter egyeztetett Giorgia Melonival, szóba került a trieszti magyar kikötő is
stories.summary: A leendő miniszterelnök azt írta, sikeres és előremutató megbeszélést folytatott az olasz miniszterelnökkel, akit meg is hívott Magyarországra.

stories.title: Felfoghatatlan összegek nehezen értelmezhető projektekre – NKA-képbehozó
stories.summary: Mága Zoltán fotósa mégis mire kapott 500 milliót? Baán László és Vidnyánszky Attila tényleg nem látta az általuk jóváhagyott beszámolót? És ki az a Krucsainé Herter Anikó? Nem csodáljuk, ha kezd elveszni a Nemzeti Kulturális Alap körüli botrány részleteiben. Képbehozó cikkünkben nemcsak a fideszes celebeknek és szervezeteknek titokban kiszórt 17 milliárd útját meséljük el, de elmagyarázzuk a szervezet működésének alapjait is.

stories.title: ECFR: More than half of Hungarians reluctant to quit Russian energy
stories.summary: The survey found that most Hungarians expect Péter Magyar to carry out reform at home, and are divided on the subject of Ukraine.

stories.title: Reagált a külügy Palkovics kijelentésére, miszerint a kormányüléseken többször tárgyalták az akkugyárak szennyezéseit
stories.summary: A KKM szerint ez nem áll ellentmondásban Szijjártó korábbi kijelentéseivel.

stories.title: Péter Magyar met with Giorgia Meloni, the Hungarian port in Trieste among subjects discussed
stories.summary: The incoming Hungarian PM described the talks as successful and forward-looking, and said he had invited the Italian leader to Hungary.

stories.title: Telex Bécsben – Választások után, rendszerváltás előtt
stories.summary: Merre tovább, Magyarország? Optimistább a hangulat, milyen irányba mutat az alakuló kormány kommunikációja? Erről is beszélgetünk Bécsben május 26-án.

stories.title: Magyarországon a legmagasabb a fogvatartottak aránya az EU-ban
stories.summary: Az Eurostat szerint 2024-ben az uniós tagállamok többségében enyhén emelkedett a fogvatartottak száma az előző évhez képest.

stories.title: Aprópénzből csináltak BEK-győztes csapatot
stories.summary: Május 30-án a sorozat történetében először rendezheti meg Budapest a Bajnokok Ligája döntőjét, a Puskás Arénában ünnepelhet Európa legjobb futballcsapata. A fináléig mindennap visszatekintünk a döntők egy-egy nagy pillanatára, egyéniségére, érdekességére. A 35 évvel ezelőtt Bariban rendezett meccsel és a Crvena zvezda győzelmével kezdjük. BL-sztorik, első rész.

stories.title: Does the name of Hungary’s next PM really translate as Peter Hungarian?
stories.summary: In short: yes. And although his party’s election victory a few weeks ago was truly unprecedented, his last name is not nearly as unique as it may sound to foreign ears.

stories.title: Elfogták a Magyar Nemzeti Vagyonkezelő volt főigazgatóját, őrizetbe is vették
stories.summary: Egy 2014-es kölcsönügyletben két és fél milliárd forintos hűtlen kezelés megalapozott gyanúja miatt hallgatták ki.

stories.title: Giorgia Meloni videót posztolt arról, ahogy fogadta Magyar Pétert Rómában
stories.summary: Meloni szerint szándékukban áll tovább erősíteni az olasz–magyar kapcsolatokat.

stories.title: Hungary's new government pushes for euro by 2030
stories.summary: Hungary's new leadership wants the country to adopt the euro by the end of the decade while repairing strained ties with Brussels. But with a weak economy and tight deadlines, experts warn the path will be steep.

stories.title: Romania's government falls, far-right firewall crumbles
stories.summary: Romania's Social Democrats have joined forces with the far right to oust the pro-reform government of Ilie Bolojan. The economic impact will be serious; the political cost high. Is the far right on the brink of power?

stories.title: Romania's government collapses after Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan loses confidence vote
stories.summary: Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan has been voted out of office by lawmakers. Romania's coalition was driven apart by unpopular austerity measures, with the Social Democrats leaving and moving to oust the PM.

stories.title: Trump sets two-month deadline for EU to enact trade deal
stories.summary: President Donald Trump set a two-month deadline for the European Union to implement a trade deal with the U.S., before he increases tariffs to “much higher levels.” Trump doubled down on his threat to levy higher tariffs on the EU in  a social media post on Thursday , as he continues to push European leaders to enact the terms of the trade accord reached in Scotland last year. The president said he relayed a July 4th deadline for the agreement during a phone call with European Commission Pr

stories.title: US and Iran launch attacks amid tenuous ceasefire
stories.summary: U.S. and Iranian forces exchanged strikes on land and at sea Thursday as President Donald Trump pursues a comprehensive deal to end the war. Central Command said U.S. forces were responding with “self-defense strikes” in the country after Iranian forces launched missiles, drones and small boats at three Navy destroyers in the Strait of Hormuz, the strategic waterway through which 20 percent of the world’s oil passes. Centcom said it had eliminated threats to the Navy ships in portraying the epis

stories.title: Man gets life in prison for firebombing pro-Israel demonstrators in Colorado
stories.summary: One person died and a dozen others were injured in the attack, during a June 2025 demonstration in Boulder A man was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole after pleading guilty on Thursday to killing one person and injuring a dozen others in a 2025 firebombing attack on a demonstration in Boulder, Colorado, in support of Israeli hostages in Gaza. Mohamed Sabry Soliman looked down at a desk throughout the sentencing at the Boulder district court. He has meanwhile pleaded n

stories.title: Trump-ally Roger Stone condemned for providing lobbying services to Myanmar’s military junta
stories.summary: Stone being paid $50,000 a month to ‘rebuild’ relations between Washington and Myanmar’s military-backed government US lobbyist Roger Stone, a longtime friend and ally of Donald Trump, has been condemned for accepting $50,000 a month to “rebuild” relations between Washington and Myanmar’s military-backed government. Myanmar’s leaders have been internationally isolated since seizing power in a coup in 2021 , and have repeatedly been accused of atrocities that may amount to war crimes . Activists

stories.title: US says it struck targets in Iran after attack on warships
stories.summary: The US military said it targeted Iranian military facilities after Iran attacked three US destroyers transiting the Strait of Hormuz. President Donald Trump called the US strikes against Iran a "love tap."

stories.title: UK to summon Chinese ambassador after convictions over spying on Hong Kong dissidents
stories.summary: The two men were found to have carried out intel gathering missions on political dissidents residing in the UK wanted by Chinese authorities. One witness reported the pair threatened to arrest him on foreign soil due to his continued opposition.

stories.title: US trade court rules Trump’s replacement tariffs illegal
stories.summary: A federal court on Thursday ruled that President Donald Trump unlawfully used Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 to impose a 10 percent global tariff — a backup plan the president implemented after the Supreme Court struck down his more sweeping worldwide tariffs earlier this year. A divided three-judge panel on the U.S. Court of International Trade  concluded that Trump’s Plan B was similarly unlawful . And the two judges in the majority barred the administration from collecting the duti

stories.title: Two men first in British history to be found guilty of spying for China
stories.summary: Chi Leung Wai and Chung Biu Yuen convicted over surveillance of dissidents in ‘shadow policing’ operation A UK Border Force officer and Hong Kong trade official based in London have been found guilty of spying for China and surveilling dissidents through a “shadow policing” operation. stories.title: Trump shelved ‘Project Freedom’ after Saudis refused use of bases and airspace
stories.summary: Riyadh told White House it would deny access for operation to provide tankers military escort through strait of Hormuz Middle East crisis – live updates A refusal by Saudi Arabia to allow the US to use its bases and airspace to provide a military escort for oil tankers passing through the strait of Hormuz lay behind Donald Trump’s decision to shelve the plan days after it had been launched. Riyadh told the White House it would not allow its Prince Sultan airbase to be used to mount the operation

stories.title: Polls close in elections across England, Scotland and Wales
stories.summary: Results of elections for councils in England, the Senedd in Wales and the Scottish parliament could transform Great Britain’s political landscape 2026 election results: latest from local, Scottish and Welsh votes Polls have closed across England, Scotland and Wales for local, mayoral and parliamentary elections, with the first results to be announced within hours. More than 30 million people across Britain were given the opportunity to vote on Thursday in what is widely seen as the biggest test

stories.title: Man charged after allegedly threatening Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor
stories.summary: Norfolk police charge 39-year-old from Suffolk, who will appear in court later A man has been charged after allegedly threatening Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor near his new home on the Sandringham Estate. The former Duke of York was out walking his dogs when the incident occurred in Wolferton, close to his Marsh Farm property, shortly after 7.30pm on Wednesday, the Telegraph reported previously. stories.title: Woman jailed in Somalia for peaceful protest ‘stripped, kicked and beaten’
stories.summary: In an exclusive interview from prison, Sadia Moalim Ali, a 27-year-old rickshaw driver, tells of her treatment after being arrested for demonstrating against the government A woman being held in prison in Somalia for taking part in peaceful protests has described how she was tortured by her guards. Sadia Moalim Ali, 27, told the Guardian she was stripped naked by two male guards in a room monitored by CCTV, kicked, beaten with a baton and left for two days in a small cell without food. stories.title: Tensions high in West Bengal after BJP aide shot dead and hundreds arrested
stories.summary: Turmoil and violence rocks state after prime minister Narendra Modi’s party claimed victory in legislative election Tensions have been high in the Indian state of West Bengal after a top political aide from Narendra Modi’s party was shot dead in the street and hundreds were arrested as violence broke out following elections this week. The prime minister’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) claimed victory in the West Bengal elections on Monday , defeating Trinamool Congress (TMC), which had ruled over

stories.title: War and peace: key themes of Pope Leo's first year in office
stories.summary: One year after his election, Pope Leo XIV is regarded as an important counterweight to US President Trump. His intended direction for the Catholic Church remains unclear.

stories.title: US court deals fresh blow to Trump's 10% global tariff policy
stories.summary: A US federal court said President Donald Trump overstepped his authority when he invoked a section of a 1974 trade law to enact the 10% global tariff policy.

stories.title: Rwandan singer dies as he was being released from prison
stories.summary: The former university lecturer was a vocal critic of the government and ruling Rwandan Patriotic Front party.

stories.title: Iran fires at 'enemy units' in Strait of Hormuz, state-run media claim
stories.summary: Iran said it launched missiles at "enemy" forces in the Strait of Hormuz after a claimed US attack on an oil tanker, and reported shooting down several drones.

stories.title: Anti-war protests rock Japan as PM pushes for stronger defence
stories.summary: Japan's largest anti-war protests in decades highlight a population divided over changes to its pacifist constitution.

stories.title: One year after India-Pakistan conflict, ceasefire holds - but little else does
stories.summary: A year after their military conflict, India and Pakistan avoid war, but diplomacy and trust remain frozen.

stories.title: Trump gives EU ultimatum deadline to approve trade deal with US
stories.summary: Trump's deadline to the European Union came as a trade court ruled his global tariff policy violated US law.

stories.title: EU to appoint Jukka Salovaara as new ambassador to UK
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Union will appoint a senior Finnish diplomat as its new ambassador to the United Kingdom, as British Prime Minister Keir Starmer pushes for a new rapprochement with the bloc. Jukka Salovaara, who has served as state secretary in Finland’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs, is expected to take the role of EU ambassador in London, three EU diplomats and officials familiar with the confirmation process, granted anonymity to discuss the as-yet-announced appointment, told POLI

stories.summary: Ivo Daalder, a former U.S. ambassador to NATO, is a senior fellow at Harvard University’s Belfer Center and host of the weekly podcast “World Review with Ivo Daalder.” He writes POLITICO’s From Across the Pond column . U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio traveled to Rome yesterday, looking to repair relations with both the Pope and the Italian government after their spats with President Donald Trump over the war in Iran. stories.title: Vatican stresses need for peace as Rubio meets pope amid strained relations
stories.summary: US secretary of state speaks with Leo and officials after tensions following attacks by Trump over Iran war The Vatican has said it raised the “need to work tirelessly in favour of peace” in talks with the US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, who is in Rome on a trip widely regarded as an effort to ease tensions after Donald Trump’s repeated criticisms of Pope Leo. Amid unprecedented strain on relations between the Holy See and Washington, Rubio was received by the pope on Thursday at the Apostol

stories.title: US and Iran close to temporary truce, Pakistani officials claim
stories.summary: Latest briefings float hope of basic deal that would keep peace and free shipping while further negotiations continue Middle East crisis – live updates The US and Iran are close to a temporary agreement to halt the war in the Middle East, officials in Pakistan claimed on Thursday, as diplomatic activity gathered fresh momentum after a near breakdown of the current ceasefire earlier this week. stories.title: Two Britons evacuated from hantavirus-hit ship ‘improving’ in hospital
stories.summary: Man, 69, is in intensive care in Johannesburg, while expedition guide Martin Anstee, 56, receiving care in Netherlands What is hantavirus? Two Britons who were medically evacuated from the hantavirus -hit cruise ship are improving, global health officials have said. A British passenger, understood to be a 69-year-old man, was taken to South Africa on 27 April and is receiving care at a private health facility in Sandton, Johannesburg. Continue reading...

stories.title: Bonnie Tyler in induced coma after emergency intestinal surgery
stories.summary: Spokesperson for Welsh singer shared further update on her health after she was rushed to hospital in Faro, Portugal Bonnie Tyler has been placed into an induced coma to aid her recovery after emergency intestinal surgery, a spokesperson for the singer has said. It was revealed on Wednesday that the 74-year-old had been rushed to a hospital near her home in Faro, Portugal, for the procedure, and was recuperating. Continue reading...

stories.title: GameStop CEO opens eBay storefront to pay for potential eBay acquisition
stories.summary: Ryan Cohen selling tube socks and baseball cards after offering $55.5bn, although source of funds remains unclear The CEO of GameStop , Ryan Cohen, said he was selling vintage video games, baseball cards, GameStop merchandise and a $14,000 pair of tube socks to help fund the company’s proposed $55.5bn acquisition of eBay. His platform of choice? eBay, of course. Continue reading...

stories.title: US trade court rules against Trump’s 10% global tariffs
stories.summary: Trump also issues new deadline for EU to implement trade deal terms before raising tariffs to ‘much higher levels’ The US trade court on Thursday ruled against Donald Trump’s latest 10% global tariffs, finding across-the-board tariffs were not justified under a 1970s trade law. The US court of international trade ruled in favor of small businesses that challenged the tariffs, which took effect on 24 February. The ruling was 2-1, with one judge saying it was premature to grant victory to the smal

stories.title: Trump gives EU until 4 July to ratify trade deal or face ‘much higher’ tariffs
stories.summary: Trump sets Fourth of July deadline as he vents impatience at speed of EU’s implementation Donald Trump has said the EU must ratify its trade deal with the US by 4 July or face “much higher” tariffs, after European officials fell short of agreement on the pact. Trump said he spoke to the EU chief, Ursula von der Leyen, about the issue and, he posted on his Truth Social platform, “agreed to give her until our Country’s 250th Birthday or, unfortunately, their Tariffs would immediately jump to much

stories.title: US-Iran ceasefire under threat after exchange of strikes in strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Donald Trump says the ceasefire remains in place despite the strikes, with Iranian TV saying the situation is ‘back to normal’ The United States and Iran exchanged fire late on Thursday in the most serious test yet of their month-long ceasefire. Iran accused the US of violating the ceasefire by targeting two ships at the strait of Hormuz and attacking civilian areas, as the US insisted it struck in retaliation. Continue reading...

stories.title: Solomon Islands to get new leader after PM ousted in no-confidence vote
stories.summary: Jeremiah Manele toppled after months of political upheaval in the nation seen as one of China’s closest partners in the Pacific Solomon Islands prime minister Jeremiah Manele lost power in a no-confidence vote in the South Pacific country’s parliament, ending months of political uncertainty. Parliament was adjourned to allow the governor general to make arrangements for the election of a new prime minister. Continue reading...

stories.title: 2026 election results: latest from local, Scottish and Welsh votes
stories.summary: From devolved parliaments in Scotland and Wales to councils and mayoralties in England, find out what happened in your area Latest news updates and analysis from election night Continue reading...

stories.title: Julie Bishop resigns as Australian National University chancellor
stories.summary: Former foreign minister steps down early as finance minister Katy Gallagher says embattled institution must continue ‘rebuilding trust and confidence’ The former foreign minister, Julie Bishop, has tendered her resignation from the position of chancellor at Australian National University (ANU). The finance minister, Katy Gallagher, said in a statement on Friday morning that the embattled institution would need to continue “rebuilding trust and confidence”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Archibald prize 2026: Richard Lewer’s portrait of artist Iluwanti Ken wins $100,000
stories.summary: Australia’s most prominent portrait prize was announced on Friday at the Art Gallery of NSW, where the finalist exhibition will open on Saturday Archibald prize 2026 finalists: Virginia Trioli, Jan Fran, Ahmed al-Ahmed and more – in pictures Richard Lewer has been awarded the 2026 Archibald prize for his portrait of Pitjantjatjara elder, traditional healer and senior artist Iluwanti Ken. The New Zealand-born, Melbourne-based artist – a six-time Archibald finalist – was announced as the winner of

stories.title: Australia news live: Julie Bishop resigns as ANU chancellor; Anika Wells repays $10,000 for incorrect travel claims
stories.summary: Katy Gallagher confirms Bishop’s resignation, saying ‘the challenges facing ANU did not arise overnight’. Follow updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast David Attenborough at 100: ‘I had thought I would celebrate quietly’ In a dose of good news, Sir David Attenborough is 100 today. The veteran broadcaster said he expected to celebrate quietly, but expressed gratitude for the well-wishes he had received. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Local council results are being announced in England; Scottish and Welsh parliament results are due from midday 2026 election results: latest from local, Scottish and Welsh votes We’re getting statements from some of the political parties now as we wait for results. For the Conservatives, party chairman Kevin Hollinrake said: We have run an energetic and positive campaign, showcasing that we have a clear plan to get Britain working again and that we have the team to deliver it... We know that so

stories.title: Trump gives EU until July 4 to implement trade deal
stories.summary: Trump has threatened to impose higher tariffs on the bloc if it doesn't implement the trade deal agreed last year by his deadline.

stories.title: Zelenskyy warns Russia's friends against attending parade
stories.summary: Zelenskyy said he did "not recommend" that representatives of states allied with Russia attend the May 9 parade. Russian authorities also said they were concerned about possible Ukrainian attacks.

stories.title: Shakira teases official 2026 FIFA World Cup song
stories.summary: The Colombian star unveils a clip of her singing the official World Cup 2026 song "Dai Dai" featuring Afrobeats artist Burna Boy.

stories.title: Is Russian oil becoming a lifeline for Southeast Asia?
stories.summary: With oil, gas and fertilizer imports squeezed by the Iran war, Southeast Asian governments are brushing off EU pressure and treating Russian energy as a tool of economic survival.

stories.title: Austria: Police report 3 dead in shooting in Linz, say no further danger to public
stories.summary: Police in Austria report that three people have died after gunfire was heard in the city. Local media reported a large police presence in the north of the city. Police also said there was no ongoing threat to the public.

stories.title: Australia: 3 women with IS links arrested on return home
stories.summary: The women flew back to Australia from Syria after years in a refugee camp. Children were also among the returnees.

stories.title: Superdry co-founder James Holder jailed for rape
stories.summary: James Holder's victim told her attacker he took her "choice, dignity and body".

stories.title: Rosenberg: Russia's Victory Day parade with no tanks a sign Ukraine war not going to plan
stories.summary: For the first time in nearly two decades, there will be no military hardware, just soldiers during Saturday's parade in Moscow.

stories.title: Hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship not start of pandemic, UN health agency says
stories.summary: Ther World Health Organization says it is not in the same situation as with Covid-19 because hantavirus spreads differently.

stories.title: Trump's 'irresponsible war' to blame for economic slowdown, German minister says
stories.summary: Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil blamed the US president's "irresponsible war in Iran" for harming the Germany economy.

stories.title: 'I wouldn't pay it' - Trump on USA ticket price
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says he "wouldn't pay it either" when asked about the potential ticket price to watch his country play Paraguay in their 2026 World Cup opener.

stories.title: Who is Kumanjayi Little Baby and why has her death caused outrage in Australia?
stories.summary: The BBC's Katy Watson explains the case, the cultural sensitivities and why it has caused so much anger.

stories.title: How operation to disembark passengers on virus-hit cruise will work
stories.summary: The BBC's Guy Hedgecoe reports from Tenerife, where some locals fear the ship's arrival could bring health risks.

stories.title: Five killed in huge fire at packed Mexico fairground
stories.summary: The blaze in the south-eastern city of Villahermosa erupted during a concert reportedly attended by some 135,000 people.

stories.title: Valverde cut head when he 'accidentally hit table' in Tchouameni row
stories.summary: Federico Valverde went to hospital after a dressing room row with Real Madrid team-mate Aurelien Tchouameni which left him with a head injury after he "accidentally hit a table".

stories.title: Thousands of North Koreans fought for Russia. A memorial hints at the death toll
stories.summary: An estimated 11,000 North Korean soldiers were sent to fight in Russia's war against Ukraine.

stories.title: Two Islamic-State linked Australian women charged with crimes against humanity
stories.summary: A third woman who also returned to Australia from Syria was charged with joining a terrorist organisation.

stories.title: 'I'd rather live in hiding in the US than return to Somalia'
stories.summary: Fear and uncertainty linger for Somali migrants in Minnesota despite a legal reprieve.

stories.title: Trump says US-Iran ceasefire still in place after exchange of fire in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Iran accuses the US of violating the truce, alleging it targeted an oil tanker and carried out attacks on coastal areas.

stories.title: Israeli airstrike kills 4 and injures 33 in southern Lebanon despite fragile ceasefire
stories.summary: Israeli airstrike hits house in Saksakieh despite ceasefire, killing four and injuring 33, as raids in southern Lebanon stoke fears of renewed escalation.

stories.title: Israel strikes Beirut for first time since ceasefire, reportedly killing Hezbollah commander
stories.summary: Beirut and its southern suburbs had been spared Israeli attacks since 8 April, when massive Israeli strikes across the country killed more than 350 people.

stories.title: Iranians at home divided between hope for regime change and war fears
stories.summary: People in Iran who spoke to Euronews said they were torn between hope for regime change and fear of devastation, while reflecting on US-Israeli strikes, rising hardship and fading support for war.

stories.title: Russia strikes Kharkiv residential district as drone attacks escalate
stories.summary: A major Russian drone offensive struck Kharkiv’s Novobavarskyi district and other cities, injuring civilians and destroying homes despite Ukraine’s strong air defence.

stories.title: China announces suspended death sentences for former defence ministers
stories.summary: The announcement follows recent ousting of several top officials in China's military.

stories.title: US underlines 'strong relationship' with Vatican as Rubio meets pope after Trump criticism
stories.summary: Rubio's visit comes after US President Trump accused the pope of being "weak on crime and terrible for foreign policy" after he made critical comments about the Iran war.

stories.title: Trump and his oil-and-coal oligarchy should face sanctions for their war on the environment | Alexander Hurst
stories.summary: Europe punished Russian billionaires over the war in Ukraine. It should do the same to those abetting an ecocidal regime The ecological disasters of the US-Israel war with Iran are already bad enough . The noxious smoke from bombed oil facilities, spills in the Gulf’s waters, the contamination of farmland and groundwater with toxic chemicals unleashed by explosions and their debris, the millions of additional tons of CO 2 spewed into the atmosphere. But as bad as it is, the Iran war hides anothe

stories.title: Singapore introduces caning for boys who bully others at school
stories.summary: Under new guidelines caning will only be used in schools for male students aged nine and above Male school students who bully others, including through cyberbullying, will face caning as a “last resort” under new guidelines introduced in Singapore. Male students can face up to three strokes of the cane under the new rules, which were discussed in parliament on Tuesday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Lib Dems accuse Badenoch of being willing to ‘put Farage in No 10’ after she hints she would approve council pacts – as it happened
stories.summary: Conservative leader says her party ‘are willing to work with people who will help deliver Conservative policies’ Labour has criticised Zack Polanski for saying he was concerned about how the suspect in the Golders Green stabbings was treated when he was arrested by the police. Referring to what Polanski said about this in his Today interview this morning (see 9.33am ), a Labour party spokesperson said: Our brave police ran towards a suspected terrorist and tackled him while he was still carrying

stories.title: Up to 2cm a month: Nasa keeps track as Mexico City sinks into the ground
stories.summary: Powerful radar system is providing new data on city’s subsidence, which experts hope will draw more attention to it Walking into Mexico City’s sprawling central Zócalo is a dizzying experience. At one end of the plaza, the capital’s cathedral, with its soaring spires, slumps in one direction. An attached church, known as the Metropolitan Sanctuary, tilts in the other. The nearby National Palace also seems off-kilter. The teetering of many of the capital’s historic buildings is the most visible s

stories.title: Israeli settler to go on trial over attack on French nun in Jerusalem
stories.summary: Yona Simcha Schreiber from settlement in West Bank faces charge of assault motivated by hostility towards religious group An Israeli settler suspected of kicking and wounding a French Catholic nun in Jerusalem will go on trial for assault motivated by hostility towards a religious group, Israel’s justice ministry has said. The attack on the nun, a 48-year-old researcher at Jerusalem’s French School of Biblical and Archaeological Research, occurred on Mount Zion, just outside the Old City. Contin

stories.title: Celebrated broadcaster and naturalist David Attenborough turns 100
stories.summary: The still-sprightly centenarian has been knighted twice, nominated for the 2022 Nobel Peace Prize, and is perhaps the world's most recognizable nature lover.

stories.title: UK votes in key local and regional elections
stories.summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Labour Party is expected to face major losses, perhaps spelling the end of his premiership. Reform, the Greens, Plaid Cymru and the SNP are all hoping to make gains.

stories.title: 'Dark patterns': Investigating online manipulation
stories.summary: Ireland's media regulator is investigating Facebook and Instagram on suspicion that so-called dark patterns are used to manipulate users.

stories.title: EU reaches tentative deal on simpler AI rules, plans ban on 'nudifier' apps
stories.summary: In a provisional deal, the European Union has agreed on a ban on the use of AI for creating pornographic deepfakes and sexualized imagery. It has also agreed on amending certain rules within the EU's AI Act.

stories.title: Germany news: Coalition at odds over pay rises for MPs
stories.summary: German conservatives want to hold on to automatic pay increases for lawmakers, while the center-left thinks they should skip it this year in light of the economic situation. DW has more.

stories.title: 'The threat is real.' Why the EU is treating Chinese solar tech like a security threat.
stories.summary: Europe's solar boom is powered by Chinese technology. But experts warn Chinese-made tech could threaten the continent's safety and even create blackouts. Now, Brussels is moving to reduce its dependence.

stories.title: Hantavirus: WHO assesses public health risk as low
stories.summary: The WHO said it expects more cases to emerge given the long incubation period. But its doctors said the virus was very different to COVID and did not pose the same threat. Follow DW for the latest.

stories.title: Eurovision 2026: Politics collide with a blockbuster show
stories.summary: Controversy over Israel's participation and boycotts unprecedented in scale are already weighing on the Eurovision Song Contest before it even kicks off in Vienna.

stories.title: Marco Rubio meets Pope Leo at the Vatican
stories.summary: The visit by the US secretary of state to the Vatican comes as President Donald Trump continues to disparage the Catholic leader over criticism of the Iran war.

stories.title: World Cup 'prices will drop' but too late for traveling fans
stories.summary: Among many firsts at World Cup 2026, FIFA has tried its hand at dynamic pricing and acting as a secondary seller. The tactic has seen a ticket listed for over $2 million but many games seem undersold. What's going on?

stories.title: South Africa: A timeline of recurring waves of xenophobic violence
stories.summary: For nearly two decades, South Africa has suffered recurring bouts of xenophobic attacks. From deadly riots in 2008 to the rise of Operation Dudula, thousand of foreign nationals have been displaced time and again.

stories.title: Ted Turner, media mogul who revolutionised TV news by launching CNN, dies at 87
stories.summary: The media mogul pioneered the modern 24-hour news culture when he launched the Cable News Network in 1980.

stories.title: Iran reviewing US proposal on deal to end war as Trump ramps up pressure
stories.summary: A fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran has largely held since 8 April but in-person talks between the two countries hosted by Pakistan last month failed to reach an agreement.

stories.title: Ukraine war 'led me to surrogacy' to earn money but a new law could end those plans
stories.summary: Ukraine is considering a law that would effectively ban foreigners from accessing surrogacy in the country.

stories.title: At Beirut shoreline camp, volunteer barber brings dignity with free haircuts
stories.summary: Lebanon’s displaced families are finding small moments of relief in Beirut. A volunteer barber is helping restore dignity after weeks of conflict.

stories.title: North Korea not bound by nuclear non-proliferation treaty, UN envoy says
stories.summary: North Korea has insisted that it will not give up its nuclear arsenal, describing its path as "irreversible" and vowing to strengthen its capabilities.

stories.title: Top BJP leader's aide shot dead in violence after Indian state election
stories.summary: Chandranath Rath was personal assistant to Suvendu Adhikari, front runner to become West Bengal's new chief minister.

stories.title: US driver arrested after chasing down child on bike on footpath
stories.summary: Video captures a young boy on a bike fleeing a car that mounted a footpath in Washington State.

stories.title: Israel army investigating after soldier seen desecrating Virgin Mary statue in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli military said on Thursday it "respects freedom of religion and worship, as well as holy sites and religious symbols of all religions and communities."

stories.title: DR Congo president hints at extending his term and delaying polls
stories.summary: "If the people want me to have a third term, I will accept," Tshisekedi says, with him due to step down in 2028.

stories.title: Local elections across UK test Starmer as Reform UK eyes major gains
stories.summary: UK voters began casting ballots on Thursday, 7 May, in local and devolved elections seen as the biggest political test yet for Prime Minister Keir Starmer since Labour’s landslide victory in July 2024.

stories.title: US judge releases Jeffrey Epstein's purported suicide note
stories.summary: Epstein's former cellmate claims he found the note, which has not been verified by the BBC.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV swears in 28 new Swiss Guards in Vatican ceremony
stories.summary: Twenty-eight new recruits were formally sworn into the Swiss Guards at Vatican City on Wednesday during a ceremony presided over by Pope Leo XIV.

stories.title: Iran's president says he met Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei amid persistent health rumours
stories.summary: As speculation grows over the possibility of an agreement between Iran and the US, Pezeshkian said he had met with the new Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, who is still to appear in public following his appointment.

stories.title: Disturbances and 127 arrests mar Paris party after PSG Champions League victory
stories.summary: Fans took to the streets to celebrate the Paris team's qualification for the final, but celebrations were marred by disturbances.

stories.title: Watch: Passengers told virus-hit ship 'not infectious' after first death
stories.summary: YouTuber Ruhi Çenet was on board the ship, and filmed the announcement made on 12 April.

stories.title: Tenerife resident calls docking of hantavirus ship 'reckless'
stories.summary: People on Tenerife in the Canary Islands have told the BBC they are concerned about the arrival of MV Hondius.

stories.title: South Africa condemns 'fake videos' of alleged xenophobic attacks
stories.summary: As claims of xenophobia in South Africa mount, Ghana calls on the African Union to send a fact-finding mission.

stories.title: Video shows United Airlines plane striking truck in New Jersey, injuring driver
stories.summary: A United Airlines plane struck a light pole and a bakery truck while attempting to land at Newark Airport. Dashcam video captured debris smashing through the truck windshield. The driver suffered minor injuries, while all 231 people aboard the aircraft were unharmed.

stories.title: Middle East war will slow down economic growth in Africa as cost of living crisis soars, IMF warns
stories.summary: According to the IMF's report, regional growth is expected to decline by 0.3 percentage points, and some 20 million people in Africa now face the risk of food insecurity as the Iran war raises energy and food prices.

stories.title: Orphaned baby hippo to be hand-reared by keepers at Kenya sanctuary
stories.summary: The baby hippo, which has been named Bumpy, was found clinging to its lifeless mother at a lake.

stories.title: Fears of renewed Gaza war as Hamas disarmament talks stall
stories.summary: Israeli media reports suggest Israel is preparing to resume fighting because of the impasse in talks with Hamas.

stories.title: Tenerife locals 'concerned but calm' as hantavirus-hit cruise ship approaches
stories.summary: Residents in southern Tenerife say it is “business as usual” despite concerns over a cruise ship linked to a hantavirus outbreak. Officials report no symptomatic cases on board, while tourism activity and bookings remain largely unaffected.

stories.title: How worried should we be about hantavirus?
stories.summary: Some passengers potentially exposed to hantavirus have already taken flights to numerous countries.

stories.summary: A női kosárlabda pécsi bajnokcsapata nem tud elindulni az Euroligában.

stories.title: Ne az legyen, hogy köszi a kampányt, és majd találkozunk négy év múlva
stories.summary: A jelek szerint a Tisza vezetése sem tudja még pontosan, hogy mihez kezdjen a Tisza Szigetekkel a Fidesz leváltása után. Bár kaptak egy kérdőívet, a tagok szerint most épp légüres térben vannak, és néha feszélyezve hívogatják egymást, hogy akkor most mi van. Néhányuk szerint ők lehetnének a kapocs a kormány és a szavazók között. Más azt sem tartja kizártnak, hogy idővel a szigetek függetleníthetik magukat a Tiszától.

stories.title: Lemond az igazságügyi miniszter-jelöltségről Melléthei-Barna Márton
stories.summary: Cikkünk frissül...

stories.summary: Vészits Andrea regényének főhőse megtanulja, hogy nem kell félni a kutyáktól, a macskáktól, a sötét éjszakáktól és attól sem, ha valami nagyon más, mint ahogy azt otthon megszokta. Kritika.

stories.summary: Szerinte ezt ki kellene vizsgálni, bár azt állítja, ez csak a jéghegy csúcsa.

stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök elégedetlen azzal, hogy az Európai Parlament még nem véglegesítette a megállapodást.

stories.summary: A CNN nemrég oknyomozó cikket közölt azokról az online közösségekről, amelyekben férfiak egymásnak tanítják, hogy hogyan erőszakolhatják meg észrevétlenül a párjukat. A Motherless és a Telegram oldalak platformot adnak a potenciális elkövetőknek, akik aztán évtizedekre tönkreteszik az áldozataikat.

stories.title: Harc indult a használt sütőolajért, összeveszett a Mohu és a Biofilter
stories.summary: A sült krumpli használt olaja más jogi szabályozás alá eshet, mint a sült húsé, de a Mohu ezt vitatja, és perelni fog az ügyben.

stories.summary: Leading newspaper La Nación calls US’s barring of board members ‘indirect attack on press freedom’ The US state department has cancelled tourist visas for more than half of the board members of Costa Rica’s leading national newspaper, La Nación, which has been a critical voice against the country’s president, Rodrigo Chaves, an ally of Donald Trump. During Chaves’s 2022 presidential campaign, La Nación published several articles documenting allegations of sexual harassment against him that had f

stories.title: US says migration has made Europe an ‘incubator’ for terrorism in new counter-terrorism strategy
stories.summary: The 16-page report was led by Trump-ally Sebastian Gorka, and places drug cartels in the Americas at the centre of counter-terrorism efforts The Trump administration has accused Europe of being an “incubator” for terrorism fuelled by mass migration, in a new counter-terrorism strategy unveiled on Wednesday. The strategy also focuses on rooting out “violent left-wing extremists” including “radically pro-transgender” groups, as Trump’s conservative administration steps up its political attacks on

stories.title: Fire at commercial complex near Tehran kills 11 and injures dozens
stories.summary: Iranian media reported on Wednesday that a fire at a shopping centre near the capital left 11 people dead and dozens of others injured. The prosecutor's office has called for an investigation into the incident to identify possible culprits, state-run media reported.

stories.title: 'We can and we will strike': Iran threatens European military bases amid Hormuz escalation
stories.summary: Iran attacked ships in Hormuz and struck the UAE again as hardliners threatened that European bases could become legitimate targets. Tehran also issued its own Strait of Hormuz navigation map, warning US Navy ships and oil tankers from crossing.

stories.title: Mexico nightclub’s $300 cover charge for US citizens captures popular mood
stories.summary: Owner of Japan nightclub says ‘This is a response to a year of insults directed at us – as a country – by the United States’ A Mexico City nightclub has gone viral for charging Americans a nearly $300 cover charge, while citizens from any other country pay just $20 for access, and Mexicans and other Latin Americans pay only $14. The Instagram announcement from the nightclub Japan in the Roma Norte neighborhood has been liked over 26,000 times and received more than 200 comments, mostly supportin

stories.title: ‘Defeated by conspiracy’: West Bengal chief minister refuses to resign after election loss
stories.summary: Mamata Banerjee says Narendra Modi’s party ‘forcefully captured’ election that ended her party’s 15 years in power A political showdown is taking place in the Indian state of West Bengal as the chief minister, Mamata Banerjee, India’s most powerful female politician, has refused to resign after she lost elections to the prime minister’s party this week. Narendra Modi’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) won an overwhelming victory on Monday in state elections in West Bengal, where Banerjee and her Tri

stories.title: Three evacuated from hantavirus-hit ship as Spain says vessel can dock
stories.summary: British guide Martin Anstee among those evacuated from MV Hondius, which is now heading for Canary Islands Explainer: What is hantavirus? Three people with suspected hantavirus have been medically evacuated from a cruise ship. They include a British man who was an expedition guide onboard the ship, the MV Hondius. He was named on Wednesday evening as Martin Anstee, 56. Continue reading...

Argentina, where the cruise to Antarctica departed, is consistently ranked by the World Health Organization (WHO) as

stories.summary: A volt ipari miniszter szerint több alkalommal felmerült a kormányban, hogy mi történik az akkugyárakban.

stories.title: A tudományos közeg mesteri átverését hozta össze egy önhitt francia főiskolai tanár
stories.summary: Florent Montaclair Nobel-díjasokkal együtt ünnepelt, pedig csak egy maga által kitalált tudományos szervezet díjazottja volt. Később valódi tudósokkal hitelesítette szervezetét, de nagyravágyása lebuktatta.

stories.title: Zalaegerszegre is került az azbeszttel szennyezett ausztriai kőzúzalékból
stories.summary: A város polgármestere, Balaicz Zoltán bejelentette, lezárnak egy parkolót.

stories.title: A Tények legalább arra jó volt, hogy egy ország láthatta, mit gondol a Fidesz a sajtóról
stories.summary: Közel 30 év után vége a Tényeknek. Egyáltalán nem objektív búcsú péniszszakértőktől, majdnem fejre eső kutyáktól és potenciális anyagyilkos, de közben rejtőzködő milliárdosoktól.

stories.title: Házkutatást tartottak a bátonyterenyei önkormányzat épületében, kommandósok vonultak ki a volt fideszes alpolgármesterhez
stories.summary: A város polgármestere szerint az eljárás során nem vettek őrizetbe senkit.

stories.title: Két embert lelőttek Linzben, a tettes magával is végzett
stories.summary: Az osztrák rendőrség szóvivője megerősítette, hogy megtalálták a gyilkos fegyvert.

stories.title: Ezer milliárd forintos állami szerződésállomány landolt Balásy cégeinél 11 év alatt
stories.summary: 2015 óta a Nemzeti Kommunikációs Hivatal összesen 1348 milliárd forintról szóló megállapodást írt alá 2025 végéig, ebből 1063 milliárd jutott Balásy cégeinek vagy az általuk vezetett konzorciumoknak.

stories.title: Here's what happens when cities kick out cars
stories.summary: A citizen-led campaign in Berlin is collecting signatures to create a car‑free center in the German capital. Other European cities show what happens when streets are built for people.

stories.title: How Rosalia became a global pop force
stories.summary: From a small town in Spain to sold-out global arenas, Spanish singer Rosalia has built her career with precision rather than luck. How did she do it?

stories.title: What the Strait of Hormuz fuel crisis means for air travel
stories.summary: Does travel insurance cover flight cancellations caused by the Iran war? Travelers could face major disruption as the jet fuel crisis threatens summer flights.

stories.title: Venice Biennale in crisis: The controversies explained
stories.summary: Following the resignation of the jury over Russia's and Israel's disputed participation, the traditional Golden Lion will not be awarded this year.

stories.title: How close is the United Kingdom to breaking up?
stories.summary: The May 7 elections could see nationalist gains in Scotland and Wales, while Sinn Fein already leads in Northern Ireland. Is the UK's unity at risk?

stories.title: Germany news: Far-right youth groups raided by police
stories.summary: The groups are considered to be prone to violence and are accused by prosecutors of forming a criminal organization. Meanwhile, Friedrich Merz has completed his first year as chancellor.

stories.title: COVID pioneer BioNTech: The fall of Germany's pandemic hero
stories.summary: BioNTech, once celebrated for its COVID-19 vaccines, is grappling with falling demand, restructuring and the departure of its founders. Can its ambitious cancer trials turn it from a one-hit wonder into a biotech giant?

stories.title: Germany cracks down on neo-Nazi networks
stories.summary: Police and prosecutors have launched a nationwide crackdown on young right-wing extremists. They are alleged to have formed criminal organizations and attacked members of the left-wing scene.

stories.title: Trump renews feud with Pope Leo XIV days ahead of planned Marco Rubio visit to the Vatican
stories.summary: In an interview with US media outlets, Trump said Pope Leo XIV’s words are helping Iran and making the world “less safe”, slamming him for his stance which Trump perceives to mean indifference about Tehran acquiring nuclear weapons.

stories.title: Top Trump advisers urge China to leverage Iran ties to reopen Strait of Hormuz to global traffic
stories.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent have both expressed a need for China to leverage its tight relations with Tehran to reopen the strategic Strait of Hormuz for “their own interests”.

stories.title: To stay or risk the 'Road of Death' - Ukrainian civilians trapped in frontline city
stories.summary: People in the Ukrainian city of Oleshky say they have been cut off from fresh supplies of food or medicine for months.

stories.title: Russian guided bomb attacks kill at least 26 civilians across Ukraine
stories.summary: Ukraine said at least 26 people were killed in Russian strikes on Tuesday 5 May 2026, with the heaviest losses in Zaporizhzhia and Kramatorsk.

stories.title: Watch: The Lebanese neighbourhoods shattered by Israel's ten minute bombing raid
stories.summary: The BBC’s Nawal Al-Maghafi has been piecing together what happened on 8 April 2026, after one of the deadliest chapters in the country’s recent history.

stories.title: Mexico marks 164 years since Battle of Puebla with historic reenactment
stories.summary: Mexico marked the 164th anniversary of the Battle of Puebla on Tuesday 5 May 2026, with hundreds of participants recreating the 1862 clash in Mexico City.

stories.title: North Korea drops references to unification with South from constitution, Seoul says
stories.summary: China has long been the country's main economic backer, though Pyongyang has drawn closer to Moscow since it launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

stories.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guard says passage through Strait of Hormuz will be ensured
stories.summary: Earlier on Wednesday, Trump said the US would pause its mission to escort commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz barely two days after it began doing so, citing a desire to reach a peace deal.

stories.title: United Nations demands Israel 'immediately' release two Gaza aid flotilla activists
stories.summary: The flotilla's vessels set sail from France, Spain and Italy with the aim of breaking Israel's blockade of Gaza and delivering humanitarian aid to the war-ravaged Palestinian territory.

stories.title: China calls for Strait to be reopened 'as soon as possible' in Iran talks
stories.summary: Wang Yi is holding talks with Iran's foreign minister Aragchi who is on his first trip to China since the war began.

stories.title: German police raid neo-Nazi criminal youth groups
stories.summary: Police search locations in 12 states, mainly in the East and South of the country in what prosecutors say is a targeted raid.

stories.title: Russia ignores Ukraine's unilateral ceasefire and attacks kindergarten
stories.summary: Ukraine's president says Russia has "spurned the ceasefire" and Kyiv is deciding on further action to take.

stories.title: Massive Alaska megatsunami was second largest ever recorded
stories.summary: New research suggests glacier melt driven by climate change is increasing the risk of giant waves.

stories.title: Italy: Femen and Pussy Riot protest Russia’s return to Venice Biennale
stories.summary: The 2026 Venice Biennale opened press previews on Wednesday with protests outside the Russian pavilion, marking Moscow’s return since the 2022 invasion of Ukraine.

stories.title: Cape Verde: three evacuated from MV Hondius over suspected hantavirus
stories.summary: Netherlands authorities have coordinated the medical evacuation of three people from the MV Hondius, including one Dutch passenger and two crew members, a Dutch and a British national. The patients, suspected of having a hantavirus infection, are being transferred to specialised hospitals.

stories.title: Outspoken CNN founder Ted Turner dies at age 87
stories.summary: Ted Turner was considered a pioneer in the news business, which he transformed after the launch of CNN in 1980.

stories.title: Trump's hopes for an Iran peace deal come with caveats
stories.summary: There are signs of fresh momentum to end the war, but Trump himself has injected a note of caution.

stories.title: Eight killed in Iran shopping centre fire as probe targets builder
stories.summary: Eight people died and 36 were injured after a huge fire engulfed a shopping centre near tehran, prompting a criminal investigation.
